## C14608 - A1 - Information Security Standards Requirements Assessments CBS Group FINAL.docx

*File: `C14608 - A1 - Information Security Standards Requirements Assessments CBS Group FINAL.docx`*

Third-Party Information Security Standards

Requirements Assessment (ISSRA)

Completed Response

OFFICIAL

Respondent Details

Supporting Evidence Provided

The following supporting document is provided with this ISSRA submission:

• CBS Group Information Security Manual (Version 1.0)

This Manual serves as the CBS Group Information Security Policy and addresses all domains covered by the ISSRA. A cross-reference matrix mapping each ISSRA question to the relevant Manual section is included as Appendix A of the Manual.

ISSRA Questionnaire — CBS Group Response

All references to “Manual s.X.X” refer to the CBS Group Information Security Manual provided with this submission.

Note: Questions 21, 40–43 are not present in the ISSRA v1.2 questionnaire (numbering gaps in the original form). All other questions have been addressed.

| 1 | Does your organisation agree to conduct information security assessments as requested by the department as part of the RFx response, at no additional cost to the department? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group agrees to participate in information security assessments requested by clients as part of procurement (RFx), engagement onboarding, during the engagement, and following any significant organisational change, at no additional cost to the client. Refer: CBS Group Information Security Manual, s.1.7.1. |

| Governance | Governance | Governance | Governance |

| 2 | Does your organisation have a maintained Information Risk Management Policy? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group maintains a formal Information Risk Management Policy (Manual s.1.4). The policy adopts a risk-based approach aligned with ISO 31000 and the Victorian Government Risk Management Framework (VGRMF). It covers risk identification (s.1.4.1), risk assessment using a 5×5 risk matrix (s.1.4.2), risk treatment strategies including avoidance, reduction, transfer and acceptance (s.1.4.3), and risk monitoring with quarterly reviews by the Information Security Officer and annual reviews by the Director (s.1.4.4). An Information Security Risk Register is maintained. A copy of the policy is available upon request. |

| 3 | Does your organisation have an Information Security Policy, which is reviewed regularly (annually)? | Yes | Yes. The CBS Group Information Security Manual serves as our Information Security Policy. It establishes our information security management framework, defines policies, procedures and controls protecting the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information. The Manual is reviewed annually by the Information Security Officer with approval by the Director (s.1.9.1). Ad hoc reviews are triggered by significant incidents, technology changes, new client requirements, legislative changes, or restructuring (s.1.9.2). The Manual is aligned with ISO/IEC 27001:2022, the Australian Government ISM, ASD Essential Eight, and VPDSS v2.0 (s.1.3). A copy is attached with this submission. |

| 4 | Does your organisation formally define information security roles and responsibilities? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group formally defines the following information security roles (Manual s.1.5): (1) Director/COO (Information Security Sponsor) — accountable for information security, approves the Manual, sets risk appetite. (2) Information Security Officer (ISO) — day-to-day management of the security framework, conducts assessments, manages access provisioning/deprovisioning, oversees incident response, coordinates training, quarterly access reviews. (3) IT Systems Administrator — manages M365 tenant, Entra ID, endpoint management, implements technical controls, monitors alerts. (4) All Personnel — policy compliance, training completion, incident reporting, credential protection, data handling. All staff are made aware of their security-related duties during onboarding and through annual training. |

| 5 | Does your organisation have a cybersecurity program, and is there a process that provides oversight of the program? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group maintains a structured cybersecurity program governed by the Information Security Manual (s.1.6). The program is overseen by the Director with operational management by the ISO. It is reviewed annually against the ASD Essential Eight Maturity Model and applicable client security standards (s.1.6.2). The annual review includes assessment of the current threat landscape, review of incidents and near-misses, evaluation of control effectiveness, update of the Risk Register, and identification of improvement actions (s.1.6.3). |

| Identification and Verification | Identification and Verification | Identification and Verification | Identification and Verification |

| 6 | Does your organisation verify staff identity and credentials before granting access to sensitive or confidential information? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group completes identity verification before any person is granted access to CBS Group or client systems (Manual s.2.2.1). A minimum of two forms of identification are verified, including at least one government-issued photo ID (passport or driver’s licence). Copies are retained securely. Additionally, qualifications, certifications and relevant employment history are verified prior to engagement (s.2.2.5). Right to work in Australia is also verified (s.2.2.2). These checks are documented and consistently applied through the onboarding process (s.2.3). |

| 7 | Does your organisation ensure staff undergo police checks and/or other required checks before staff get access to sensitive or confidential information? | Yes | Yes. A National Police Check (or equivalent criminal history check) is required for all CBS Group personnel. The check must be current (less than 12 months old) and is renewed every three years, or as required by client contracts (Manual s.2.2.3). Where engagements involve access to information relating to children, young people or families (including Victorian DH or DFFH engagements), a current Working with Children Check (WWCC) is required prior to access being granted (s.2.2.4). Client-specific checks (e.g., security clearances) are also completed where required (s.2.2.6). The process is formally documented and consistently applied. |

| Access Management | Access Management | Access Management | Access Management |

| 8 | Does your organisation have a documented process for provisioning, modifying, suspending, de-provisioning, and re-validating access to systems and information? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group maintains documented access management procedures covering the full access lifecycle (Manual Chapter 3). Access provisioning requires formal requests to the ISO including business justification, required access level and expected duration (s.3.3.1). The ISO validates the request, confirms screening, and approves or rejects (s.3.3.2). Access reviews are conducted quarterly for OFFICIAL: Sensitive and above, and bi-annually for OFFICIAL systems (s.3.6). Deprovisioning follows classification-based timelines: PROTECTED within 1 business day, OFFICIAL: Sensitive within 3 business days, OFFICIAL within 5 business days (s.3.7). All access is governed by least privilege and need-to-know principles (s.3.2). |

| 9 | Do you make your staff and contractors aware that they should not share their credentials? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group personnel are informed during onboarding, security training and through the Information Security Manual that credentials (usernames and passwords) must not be shared under any circumstances (Manual s.3.4.1). This is reinforced through the Acceptable Use Policy (s.6.5), which lists sharing credentials as a prohibited activity (s.6.5.1(b)). Annual refresher training covers credential security (s.9.2.1). All personnel sign a Confidentiality and Security Acknowledgement (Appendix C.1). |

| 10 | Will your organisation undertake regular access reviews of staff and contractors who work with departmental systems/information/data? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group conducts formal access reviews on a documented schedule (Manual s.3.6): quarterly for personnel with access to client systems classified OFFICIAL: Sensitive or above, and bi-annually for OFFICIAL-classified systems. Each review validates ongoing business need, verifies access levels are appropriate for current role, and identifies orphaned or excessive accounts (s.3.6.2). Outcomes are documented and retained for a minimum of two years (s.3.6.3). Access no longer required is revoked within five business days (s.3.6.4). |

| 11 | Does your organisation have a documented, formal process to manage access to systems and environments? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group has a documented access provisioning process that defines how access is requested, approved and granted (Manual s.3.3). It differentiates access levels based on user roles and applies role-based access control principles (s.3.2). The process applies across all CBS Group cloud systems (M365, Entra ID) and client environments (s.3.3.4). Privileged access is managed separately with additional controls including separate admin accounts, mandatory MFA, conditional access policies, and quarterly reviews (s.3.8). |

| 12 | Does your organisation have documented time frames for access to systems/networks/data? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group’s access management procedures include documented time frames. All access requests must specify the expected duration of access (Manual s.3.3.1). Access reviews are conducted quarterly and bi-annually per s.3.6 to re-validate ongoing need. Deprovisioning follows classification-based timelines (s.3.7): PROTECTED — 1 business day, OFFICIAL: Sensitive — 3 business days, OFFICIAL — 5 business days. Time-bound access is a standard field on the Access Request Form (Appendix C.4). |

| 13 | Does your organisation have a documented, formal process and access review schedule to confirm that a user’s access is still required? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group maintains a documented access review schedule (Manual s.3.6.1): quarterly for OFFICIAL: Sensitive and above, bi-annually for OFFICIAL. The ISO is assigned responsibility for conducting reviews. The review process covers identification of all active accounts, validation of ongoing need, verification that access levels remain appropriate, and identification of orphaned or excessive accounts (s.3.6.2). Results are documented and retained for two years (s.3.6.3). |

| 14 | Will your organisation notify the department when staff no longer require access to departmental systems/information/data? | Yes | Yes. For personnel with access to client systems, the ISO notifies the relevant client contact within contractual timeframes (Manual s.2.5.2). Where no specific timeframe is contractually defined, the following timelines apply based on data classification: PROTECTED — within 1 business day, OFFICIAL: Sensitive — within 3 business days, OFFICIAL — within 5 business days. CBS Group will also promptly notify clients of material changes that may affect the security of client information (s.1.7.3). |

| 15 | Does your organisation have set timelines for deprovisioning staff access when they leave or change roles? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group has documented deprovisioning timelines based on data classification (Manual s.3.7.1): PROTECTED information/systems — within 1 business day, OFFICIAL: Sensitive — within 3 business days, OFFICIAL — within 5 business days. The IT Systems Administrator confirms completion to the ISO, who logs the action in the access register (s.3.7.2). For client systems, the ISO notifies the client contact within applicable timelines, following the client’s deprovisioning procedures (s.3.7.3). An Offboarding Checklist (Appendix C.2) is completed for all departures. |

| 16 | Does your organisation monitor and audit access to systems, networks, and data? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group generates and retains audit logs capturing user logins/logouts, access to sensitive records, administrative actions, changes to security configurations, and failed authentication attempts (Manual s.3.9.1). Logs are retained for a minimum of 12 months within the M365 environment (s.3.9.2). The IT Systems Administrator reviews security alerts weekly and conducts monthly reviews of audit logs for anomalous activity (s.3.9.3). Audit trails are available to clients upon request for compliance verification or forensic investigation (s.3.9.4). |

| Fourth Party Management | Fourth Party Management | Fourth Party Management | Fourth Party Management |

| 17 | If your organisation intends to subcontract services to third parties, will you disclose the names and roles of those parties to the department? | Yes | Yes. Where CBS Group intends to engage subcontractors, service providers or partners who will access, process or share client information, the names and roles of those parties will be disclosed to the relevant client prior to engagement (Manual s.8.2.1). Subcontractors are subject to the same security obligations as CBS Group personnel, as outlined in the relevant client contract and this Manual (s.8.2.2). |

| 18 | Does your organisation have a due diligence process to assess your third parties for solvency and their information security management maturity prior to their engagement? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group performs due diligence on all third parties who will have access to client or sensitive information before engagement (Manual s.8.3.1). Due diligence includes assessment of the third party’s information security capability and maturity (including certifications such as ISO 27001, SOC 2, Essential Eight alignment), verification of financial solvency, and review of privacy and data handling practices. Outcomes are documented and retained (s.8.3.2). |

| 19 | Does your organisation confirm that any third parties you engage with comply with your security policies and standards? | Yes | Yes. Contracts, statements of work and engagement terms with third parties include security clauses requiring the third party to comply with CBS Group’s information security policies, comply with applicable client security requirements, notify CBS Group of any security incident, and permit security assessments (Manual s.8.4.1). Compliance is confirmed through periodic review (at least annually) or as triggered by incidents or significant changes (s.8.4.2). |

| 20 | Does your organisation conduct risk assessments on your third parties? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group assesses the information security risk from each third party based on the nature and sensitivity of information accessed, the type of access granted, the third party’s security maturity, and the availability of alternative providers (Manual s.8.5.1). Risk assessments are reviewed annually or upon material change in the third party’s engagement or security posture (s.8.5.2). |

| Data Management | Data Management | Data Management | Data Management |

| 22 | Are staff aware of the Victorian Protective Data Security Framework (VPDSF) protective markings? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group recognises and applies the VPDSF protective marking scheme when handling Victorian Government information (Manual s.4.2.1). The classifications recognised are OFFICIAL, OFFICIAL: Sensitive, and PROTECTED. Personnel are trained to recognise protective markings on documents, emails and data files, and to handle information in accordance with the controls applicable to its classification level (s.4.2.2). Training covers secure information handling including recognising and applying protective markings (s.9.5.1). |

| 23 | Does your organisation apply information handling controls based on classification level? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group applies minimum controls based on classification level (Manual s.4.3): OFFICIAL — stored on approved systems (M365), access restricted to authorised personnel, transmitted via TLS 1.2+ (s.4.3.1). OFFICIAL: Sensitive — additional need-to-know access, stored in access-controlled SharePoint/OneDrive, encrypted at rest and in transit, secure printing and disposal (s.4.3.2). PROTECTED — additional client-specific handling, access limited to named client-approved individuals (s.4.3.3). |

| 24 | Will your organisation return all departmental data upon completion or termination of the engagement? | Yes | Yes. Upon termination or completion of a client engagement, CBS Group will return all client data, records, documents and other public sector information (electronic and physical) promptly (Manual s.4.6.1). The process includes identifying, extracting and securely transferring all data. CBS Group does not retain copies of client data beyond the period required by the contract or by law (s.4.6.4). |

| 25 | Does your organisation have a process for permanent deletion or de-identification of departmental data? | Yes | Yes. Where data cannot be returned (e.g., embedded in backup systems), CBS Group permanently deletes or de-identifies the data and provides written confirmation (certificate of destruction) to the client (Manual s.4.6.2). Destruction methods are appropriate to media type: secure deletion using overwrite methods for electronic storage, cross-cut shredding for paper, and certified destruction for physical media (s.4.6.3). |

| 26 | Does your organisation restrict the use of AI tools (e.g., ChatGPT, Copilot, Gemini) with departmental data? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group personnel and subcontractors must not upload, enter or submit any client or public sector information into any AI platform, tool or model (including ChatGPT, Google Gemini, Microsoft Copilot, Claude or custom ML models) without explicit prior written consent from the relevant client (Manual s.4.4.1). This prohibition applies to public AI services, in-house AI tools, and AI features embedded in productivity software where the client has not approved their use (s.4.4.2). Where client approval is granted, the approved scope and conditions are documented and communicated to all relevant personnel (s.4.4.3). |

| Data Sharing | Data Sharing | Data Sharing | Data Sharing |

| 27 | Does your organisation have contractual controls to restrict sharing of departmental data with unauthorised parties? | Yes | Yes. Client data must not be shared with any third party, subcontractor or supplier unless explicitly authorised in the relevant contract or the client has provided separate written authorisation (Manual s.4.5.1). CBS Group maintains controls to ensure data flows to subcontractors are pre-approved and traceable (s.4.5.2). Where CBS Group intends to use a subcontractor for work involving client data, this is disclosed to the client prior to engagement (s.4.5.3). |

| 28 | Will your organisation require written authorisation before sharing departmental data with any third party? | Yes | Yes. Client data must not be shared with any third party unless the client has provided written authorisation, either through the contract or as separate approval (Manual s.4.5.1). CBS Group maintains controls to ensure all data flows to external parties are pre-approved and traceable (s.4.5.2). |

| Contract Reviews | Contract Reviews | Contract Reviews | Contract Reviews |

| 29 | Will your organisation cooperate with security assessments throughout the engagement lifecycle? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group will cooperate with security assessments at the following stages: prior to engagement commencement, during the engagement (including periodic reviews), and after any significant change to CBS Group’s organisation, systems or security posture (Manual s.1.7.2). CBS Group will promptly notify clients of material changes that may affect security, including data breaches, changes in subcontractor arrangements, service location changes, or changes to organisational control (s.1.7.3). |

| Technical Controls | Technical Controls | Technical Controls | Technical Controls |

| 30 | Is your organisation aligned to the ASD Essential Eight or equivalent cybersecurity framework? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group implements the ASD Essential Eight mitigation strategies and assesses its environment against the Essential Eight Maturity Model. CBS Group’s current target is Maturity Level Two across all eight strategies (Manual s.5.2.1). The eight strategies implemented are: application control, patch applications, configure Microsoft Office macro settings, user application hardening, restrict administrative privileges, patch operating systems, multi-factor authentication, and regular backups (s.5.2.2). An annual self-assessment against the E8 Maturity Model is conducted (s.5.2.3). |

| 31 | Does your organisation enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA)? | Yes | Yes. MFA is mandatory for all CBS Group systems and cloud services, including Microsoft 365, remote access and any system containing client or sensitive information (Manual s.3.5.1). Approved MFA methods include authenticator applications (e.g., Microsoft Authenticator) and hardware security tokens. SMS-based MFA is discouraged and only permitted where no alternative is available (s.3.5.2). MFA is enforced through Microsoft Entra ID conditional access policies (s.3.5.3). |

| 32 | Does your organisation maintain application control / a list of approved applications? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group maintains a list of approved applications for all workstations (Manual s.5.2.2(a)). Unapproved software execution is restricted through Microsoft Intune application management policies. The approved application list is reviewed and validated annually and maintained through a change management process. Only approved devices and software may be used for CBS Group or client work (s.6.2.3). |

| 33 | Does your organisation manage privileged access separately from standard user access? | Yes | Yes. Administrative (privileged) accounts are limited to the IT Systems Administrator and the ISO (Manual s.3.8.1). Privileged accounts are separate from standard user accounts — personnel with admin duties maintain two accounts: one for daily use and one for administrative tasks (s.3.8.2). Additional controls include mandatory MFA, conditional access policies restricting login to compliant devices, and restriction from email/internet browsing on admin accounts (s.3.8.3). Privileged access is reviewed quarterly and documented (s.3.8.4). |

| 34 | Does your organisation have a formal patch management process? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group maintains a formal patch management process covering operating systems and third-party applications (Manual s.5.3). Critical and high-risk security patches are applied within 48 hours of release, all other security patches within two weeks, and feature updates within one month following testing (s.5.3.2). Deployment is managed through Microsoft Intune and Windows Update for Business (s.5.3.3). Compliance status is monitored weekly (s.5.3.4). |

| 35 | Does your organisation have a formal backup management process? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group maintains a formal backup management process (Manual s.7.2). M365 data (SharePoint, OneDrive, Exchange, Teams) is backed up daily through native retention policies and a third-party backup solution. Critical business data is backed up incrementally daily and fully weekly (s.7.2.2). Backup data is stored in a geographically separate Australian data centre (s.7.2.3). All backup data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 (s.7.2.4). Restoration testing is conducted at least every six months (s.7.3.1). |

| 36 | Does your organisation have procedures for secure disposal of backup media? | Yes | Yes. Where physical backup media is used, it is stored securely, transport is tracked, and disposal is performed using certified destruction methods with certificates of destruction maintained as evidence (Manual s.7.4.1). For cloud-based backups, disposal is managed through the provider’s secure deletion processes, verified through compliance certifications (s.7.4.2). Hardware disposal follows NIST SP 800-88 guidelines with documentation in the asset register (s.6.6.1). |

| Asset Management | Asset Management | Asset Management | Asset Management |

| 37 | Does your organisation have an asset management policy? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group maintains a formal asset management policy (Manual s.6.2). The policy covers hardware and software assets throughout their lifecycle including company-issued and BYOD devices. Only approved devices and software may be used for CBS Group or client work (s.6.2.3). Company-issued devices are configured with a security baseline including BitLocker encryption, Microsoft Defender, Intune enrolment, auto-updates, and firewall enabled (s.6.3.1). Secure disposal follows documented procedures (s.6.6). |

| 38 | Does your organisation maintain an asset inventory/register? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group maintains an asset register recording all hardware and software assets including device type, make, model and serial number, assigned user, location, operating system and version, software installed, acquisition date and disposal date (Manual s.6.2.1). The register is maintained by the IT Systems Administrator and audited annually (s.6.2.2). |

| Vulnerability Management | Vulnerability Management | Vulnerability Management | Vulnerability Management |

| 39 | Does your organisation conduct regular vulnerability assessments? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group conducts regular vulnerability assessments (Manual s.5.4). External vulnerability scanning is conducted quarterly using an approved scanning tool (s.5.4.2). Internal infrastructure scanning is conducted bi-annually (s.5.4.3). Identified vulnerabilities are triaged based on risk: critical — 48 hours, high — 2 weeks, medium — 1 month, low — 3 months (s.5.4.4). A vulnerability remediation register is maintained (s.5.4.5). |

| Email Security | Email Security | Email Security | Email Security |

| 44 | Does your organisation have SPF (Sender Policy Framework) records configured? | Yes | Yes. DNS records specify which mail servers are authorised to send email on behalf of CBS Group’s domain (Manual s.5.6.1(a)). |

| 45 | Does your organisation have DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail) configured? | Yes | Yes. Outgoing emails are cryptographically signed to verify integrity using DKIM (Manual s.5.6.1(b)). |

| 46 | Does your organisation have DMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting and Conformance) configured? | Yes | Yes. A DMARC policy is published with a minimum enforcement level of “quarantine”, progressing to “reject”. DMARC reports are reviewed monthly (Manual s.5.6.1(c)). |

| 47 | Does your organisation have email security hardening measures in place (anti-phishing, anti-malware)? | Yes | Yes. Anti-phishing and anti-malware protections are enabled through Microsoft Defender for Office 365 (or equivalent), including safe links, safe attachments and impersonation protection (Manual s.5.6.2). External email warnings are applied to emails originating from outside the organisation (s.5.6.3). |

| Network Security | Network Security | Network Security | Network Security |

| 48 | Does your organisation have a network management/security policy? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group operates a cloud-first environment with network security managed through M365 and Entra ID security controls (Manual s.5.7). Controls include: perimeter controls compliant with client network requirements (s.5.7.2(a)), endpoint protection via Microsoft Defender for Endpoint with EDR (s.5.7.2(b)), conditional access policies (s.5.7.2(c)), and secure remote access via encrypted connections (VPN or HTTPS/TLS 1.2+) (s.5.7.2(d)). Security configurations are reviewed quarterly (s.5.7.3). |

| 49 | Does your organisation have intrusion detection/prevention systems (IDS/IPS)? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group leverages Microsoft Defender for Endpoint and Microsoft Sentinel (or equivalent) for intrusion detection and prevention (Manual s.5.8.1). Capabilities include real-time threat detection and alerting, behavioural analysis for anomalous activity, and automated threat response for known attack patterns. Security alerts are reviewed by the IT Systems Administrator, with critical alerts escalated immediately to the ISO (s.5.8.2). |

| 50 | Does your organisation conduct penetration testing? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group engages an independent, accredited security provider to conduct penetration testing at least annually, or following major changes to network, systems or web applications (Manual s.5.5.1). Scope includes external network assessment, cloud configuration review (M365/Entra ID), and web application testing where applicable (s.5.5.2). Findings are prioritised and remediated per s.5.4.4 timelines, with critical/high findings verified by the testing provider (s.5.5.3). |

| Security Awareness and Training | Security Awareness and Training | Security Awareness and Training | Security Awareness and Training |

| 51 | Does your organisation provide cybersecurity awareness training to staff? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group maintains a structured cybersecurity awareness training program (Manual s.9.2) covering phishing, password management, remote work practices, device security, data classification, incident reporting, AI use restrictions, and client-specific requirements. Induction training is completed by all new personnel prior to system access (s.9.3.1). Annual refresher training is mandatory (s.9.3.2). Ad hoc training is provided in response to emerging threats, policy changes, or incidents (s.9.3.3). Training records are maintained by the ISO (s.9.4). |

| 52 | Are staff trained on their obligations regarding secure handling of departmental information? | Yes | Yes. Personnel are specifically trained on secure information handling obligations including recognising and applying protective markings, secure storage and transmission practices, acceptable use requirements, and information disposal procedures (Manual s.9.5.1). This is reinforced through signed Acceptable Use acknowledgements and ongoing operational reminders. All personnel sign a Confidentiality and Security Acknowledgement (Appendix C.1). |

| Physical Security and Remote Access | Physical Security and Remote Access | Physical Security and Remote Access | Physical Security and Remote Access |

| 53 | Does your organisation have physical security controls and secure remote access arrangements? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group operates a cloud-first model. Personnel accessing CBS Group or client systems remotely do so via encrypted connections (VPN or HTTPS/TLS 1.2+) (Manual s.5.7.2(d)). Endpoint protection via Microsoft Defender for Endpoint is mandatory on all devices (s.5.7.2(b)). Conditional access policies restrict access based on device compliance, location, user risk level and sign-in risk level (s.5.7.2(c)). BYOD devices must meet minimum security baselines including encryption, screen lock, antivirus and auto-updates (s.6.4.1(b)). |

| 54 | Does your organisation have controls for BYOD (Bring Your Own Device) access? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group permits BYOD subject to: enrolment in Microsoft Intune (s.6.4.1(a)), minimum security requirements including current OS, full-disk encryption, screen lock, antivirus and auto-updates (s.6.4.1(b)), conditional access policies (s.6.4.1(c)), data containerisation via Intune application protection policies (s.6.4.1(d)), and remote wipe capability (s.6.4.1(e)). CBS Group data is removed via selective wipe upon departure (s.6.4.1(f)). |

| 55 | Does your organisation have an acceptable use policy for systems and devices? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group has documented acceptable use requirements (Manual s.6.5). The policy defines permitted use for business purposes with reasonable personal use (s.6.5.1(a)). Prohibited activities include credential sharing, installing unapproved software, disabling security controls, storing client data on personal cloud storage, and connecting to unsecured Wi-Fi without VPN (s.6.5.1(b)). CBS Group monitors system and device use for security purposes (s.6.5.1(c)). |

| Data Sovereignty | Data Sovereignty | Data Sovereignty | Data Sovereignty |

| 56 | Does your organisation store departmental data within Australian data centres only? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group stores all client and public sector information within Australian data centres unless explicit prior written approval is obtained from the relevant client (Manual s.4.8.1). CBS Group’s Microsoft 365 tenant is configured with Australian data residency (s.4.8.2). CBS Group will not store or process client information in overseas data centres or use international cloud providers for client data without client approval (s.4.8.3). |

| 57 | Does your organisation prevent offshore processing of departmental data without prior written approval? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group will not process client information offshore unless the client has provided explicit written approval (Manual s.4.8.3). Where offshore processing is proposed, CBS Group discloses full details of location(s) and providers, requests written approval, and complies with any conditions stipulated by the client (s.4.8.4). |

| Logging and Monitoring | Logging and Monitoring | Logging and Monitoring | Logging and Monitoring |

| 58 | Does your organisation maintain security audit logs? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group generates and retains audit logs capturing user logins/logouts, access to sensitive records, administrative actions, changes to security configurations, and failed authentication attempts (Manual s.3.9.1). Logs are retained for a minimum of 12 months (s.3.9.2). |

| 59 | Does your organisation review security logs regularly? | Yes | Yes. The IT Systems Administrator reviews security alerts weekly and conducts monthly reviews of audit logs for anomalous activity (Manual s.3.9.3). |

| 60 | Are audit trails available to the department upon request? | Yes | Yes. Audit trails are made available to clients upon request for compliance verification or forensic investigation (Manual s.3.9.4). |

| Incident Response and Business Continuity | Incident Response and Business Continuity | Incident Response and Business Continuity | Incident Response and Business Continuity |

| 61 | Does your organisation have a Security Incident Response Plan (SIRP), Business Continuity Plan (BCP) and Disaster Recovery Plan (DRP)? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group maintains all three plans (Manual Chapter 10): SIRP (s.10.2) — covers incident classification (Critical/High/Medium/Low), detection and reporting, triage, containment, client notification, investigation, eradication and recovery, and post-incident review within 10 business days. BCP (s.10.3) — covers critical function continuity through cloud-first architecture, geo-redundant backup, remote work capability, and communication plan. DRP (s.10.4) — covers recovery objectives (RTO: 24 hours, RPO: 24 hours), and restoration procedures including integrity verification. |

| 62 | Does your organisation acknowledge the department’s right to temporarily suspend your access during a security incident? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group acknowledges that clients may temporarily suspend CBS Group’s access to client systems during a security incident. Access will be reinstated upon the client’s satisfaction that the incident is contained and resolved. CBS Group will cooperate fully during such periods (Manual s.10.2.4). |

| 63 | Will your organisation participate in departmental incident response exercises? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group agrees to participate in client-initiated incident response exercises (e.g., tabletop exercises simulating data breaches, ransomware or service disruptions) upon request (Manual s.10.5.2). Test outcomes are documented and improvement actions tracked to completion (s.10.5.3). |

| Security Roles and Contacts | Security Roles and Contacts | Security Roles and Contacts | Security Roles and Contacts |

| 64 | Does your organisation have a designated person responsible for authorising access to departmental systems? | Yes | Yes. The Information Security Officer (ISO) serves as the designated authorising role within CBS Group for client system access (Manual s.3.3.4). The ISO validates access requests, confirms screening, and approves or rejects requests (s.3.3.2). The ISO also serves as the designated contact for client information security queries (s.1.5.2(f)). |

| 65 | Does your organisation have a designated security contact for the department? | Yes | Yes. The Information Security Officer (ISO) serves as the designated contact for client information security queries (Manual s.1.5.2(f)). Contact details will be provided to the department upon engagement commencement. |

| Incident Management and Notification | Incident Management and Notification | Incident Management and Notification | Incident Management and Notification |

| 66 | Will your organisation notify the department of security incidents affecting departmental data? | Yes | Yes. For incidents affecting client data or systems, the ISO notifies the relevant client contact immediately for Critical incidents and within 24 hours for High incidents (Manual s.10.2.3(d)). Notification includes a description of the incident, systems and data affected, containment actions taken, and expected timeline for resolution. |

| 67 | Does your organisation have defined incident classification criteria? | Yes | Yes. Incidents are classified as: Critical — confirmed data breach, ransomware, or system compromise affecting client operations; High — suspected data breach, malware, unauthorised access, loss/theft of device with client data; Medium — phishing compromise without data loss, policy violation, failed intrusion; Low — suspicious activity under investigation, near-misses (Manual s.10.2.2). |

| 68 | Does your organisation have documented incident response procedures? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group follows a structured incident response process (Manual s.10.2.3): (a) Detection and reporting — all personnel report immediately to the ISO; (b) Triage and classification; (c) Containment — isolate systems, disable accounts, block malicious IPs, preserve evidence; (d) Client notification per timelines; (e) Investigation including forensic examination; (f) Eradication and recovery; (g) Post-incident review within 10 business days covering root cause, lessons learned and recommended improvements. |

| 69 | Does your organisation conduct post-incident reviews? | Yes | Yes. A post-incident review is conducted within 10 business days of incident closure (Manual s.10.2.3(g)). The review covers timeline of events, effectiveness of response, root cause analysis, lessons learned, and recommended improvements. Findings are documented and incorporated into the Manual where applicable. |

| 70 | Does your organisation have defined notification timelines for security incidents? | Yes | Yes. Client notification timelines (Manual s.10.2.3(d)): Critical incidents — immediately; High incidents — within 24 hours. Notification includes incident description, affected systems/data, containment actions, and expected resolution timeline. |

| 71 | Does your organisation maintain an incident register? | Yes | Yes. The ISO logs all incidents in the Incident Register (Manual s.10.2.3(a)). An Incident Report Template is maintained (Appendix C.3) capturing incident ID, date/time, reporter, classification, description, affected systems/data, clients affected, containment actions, client notification status, root cause, resolution, lessons learned and closure date. |

| 72 | Does your organisation have a communication plan for security incidents? | Yes | Yes. The ISO coordinates communication with CBS Group personnel, clients, and relevant authorities (Manual s.10.3.4). For incidents affecting client data, notification includes description, affected systems/data, containment actions and expected timeline (s.10.2.3(d)). Alternate communication channels are used if primary systems are unavailable. |

| Encryption | Encryption | Encryption | Encryption |

| 73 | Does your organisation encrypt sensitive and client information at rest and in transit? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group encrypts sensitive and client information both at rest and in transit (Manual s.4.7). At rest: BitLocker full-disk encryption on all Windows devices, M365 service encryption for SharePoint/OneDrive/Exchange, AES-256 for backup data (s.4.7.2). In transit: TLS 1.2 or above for all network transmission, AES-256 VPN connections, M365 message encryption for OFFICIAL: Sensitive and above (s.4.7.3). Key management is via Microsoft’s infrastructure, with client-specific arrangements available (s.4.7.4). |

| Forensic Investigation | Forensic Investigation | Forensic Investigation | Forensic Investigation |

| 74 | Will your organisation provide access to log files and system evidence for forensic investigation upon request? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group agrees to provide log files, audit trails and system evidence to clients upon request for forensic investigation (Manual s.10.2.5). CBS Group will facilitate secure transfer or access for forensic analysis and retain logs in a manner that allows retrieval within agreed retention periods. |

| Cyber Insurance | Cyber Insurance | Cyber Insurance | Cyber Insurance |

| 75 | Does your organisation maintain an active cyber insurance policy? | Yes | Yes. CBS Group obtains cyber insurance cover on a per-engagement basis, ensuring coverage is tailored to the specific risk profile and requirements of each client engagement. The policy covers data breaches, ransomware, business interruption and third-party liabilities (Manual s.1.8.2). A certificate of insurance or policy summary will be provided to the department upon request prior to engagement commencement (s.1.8.3). |
